title: what registries does skope support?
body: Does skopeo support fetching image digest from gcr and aws?

comments.body: @vrothberg is there a version available where i can import the project as go module? i dont want a cli. Is there any way to this?

@amokkara, Skopeo is a CLI tool only and is not meant to be imported as a Go module.  Skopeo builds around the github.com/containers/image library. Feel free to have a look at the Skopeo code on how to use it.
@vrothberg  in github.com/containers/image, how to get bearer token for a registry? is there a method that i can call or it should be manually provided by user
@amokkara all registry-related code is in https://github.com/containers/image/blob/main/docker/docker_client.go but bearer tokens are not something the API exposes to the user.
@vrothberg sorry, didnt quite get it.. bearer tokens should be provided by the user explicitly?
No, bearer tokens are not something the user has to worry about at all.  Can you elaborate what you want to do?

@vrothberg  How can we provide login credentials?

currently this is how im running it 

`ref, err := docker.ParseReference("//registry-1.docker.io/library/ubuntu:latest")
		if err != nil {
			panic(err)
		}
		ctx := context.Background()
	dig,err := docker.GetDigest(ctx,&types.SystemContext{OSChoice: "linux", **DockerBearerRegistryToken**: "**<some token>**"},ref)
`

AS you see here, im providing bearer token..How else can we do this with me providing bearer token. Is there a method to call this?

And this code is for dockerhub.. is it same for google container registry?

> AS you see here, im providing bearer token..How else can we do this with me providing bearer token. Is there a method to call this?

If you have the bearer token, you can specify it there. You can also specify credentials via https://github.com/containers/image/blob/main/types/types.go#L615.
 
> And this code is for dockerhub.. is it same for google container registry?

Yes.

> docker.ParseReference("//registry-1.docker.io/library/ubuntu:latest")

This should `docker.io/library/ubuntu:latest`.  c/image will take care of the rest.

The reference to GCR is the same as you would pass when doing a `podman pull` or `docker pull`.

Please have a look at the link above and read the code. The data can be set via https://github.com/containers/image/blob/6f3c8453e1a7d8096e4536512bf883a47e68e9e0/types/types.go#L473.

An example would be:
```golang
sys := types.SystemContext{}
sys.DockerAuthConfig = types.DockerAuthConfig{Username: ..., Password: ...} // also supports identity tokens
```

@vrothberg  sorry, i understand this part. I understood how to add credentials in DockerAuthConfig.
What i wanted to know is, in GCR we wont have username and password.
We have service account. So how to use service account and access registry.
Is there any way? or is it possible to get username and password from gcr?

>  or is it possible to get username and password from gcr?

Sorry, I don't know enough about GCR internals. Did you have a look at https://cloud.google.com/container-registry/docs/advanced-authentication?

containers/image supports credential helpers and GCR one is known to work.
